steps.title: Identify the comparative signal
steps.content: The passage presents voting ages from two countries with different requirements — Panama at 18 and Isle of Man at 16. The transition should signal how these contrast with each other.

steps.title: Apply the signal to the choices
steps.content: ”By comparison” directly indicates we're contrasting the two jurisdictions' voting requirements. The other three misidentify the relationship: “Firstly” implies a series (none exists), “For example” treats the second item as exemplifying something unestablished, and “Therefore” invents causation. That matches choice B — B is correct.
